source.submodule: What Happens in a Traditional App

topicTags: What Happens in a Traditional App

content.problemStatement:
- You are working on the same application from the previous page.
- Currently: An order is placed, Payment is done using Credit Card

--- 

**Now the business introduces a new requirement:** The application should support UPI payments. Later, more payment methods may be added.

- You are asked to update the existing code to support this requirement.
- A new payment class is available:

```java
class UPIPaymentService {
    public void pay() {
        System.out.println("Payment done using UPI");
    }
}
```


The rest of the application remains unchanged.

---


**To support UPI payments without removing Credit Card payments, the following changes were made:**
 - A new UPIPaymentService class was added
 - OrderService now creates both payment service objects
 - Conditional logic (if-else) is used to decide which payment method to use at runtime

This solution allows the application to support multiple payment methods at the same time, and it works correctly.

**However, an important design issue still exists.**
